summary: The article discusses China's strategic planning for the 15th five-year plan (2026-2030), focusing on achieving global competitiveness in semiconductors and AI. It highlights the transition from 'catching up' and 'import substitution' to pioneering new technological pathways to bypass US-led export curbs and avoid redundant internal competition. It mentions specific technical goals like FDSOI technology, RISC-V, and brain-inspired computing, and and the AI ecosystem integration of data, algorithms, and computing power.
main_claims: China aims to be a global leader in science and technology by 2035
The 15th five-year plan period is a critical window for breakthroughs in AI and semiconductors
China must move from 'import substitution' to pioneering new technological pathways to overcome US export curbs
Redundant internal competition among domestic firms is wasting innovative resources
AI is a dual catalyst: it creates demand for AI chips and transforms the manufacturing process itself
The AI strategy involves breakthroughs in foundational algorithms, brain-inspired computing, and the integration of data/computing power
evidence_or_facts: National R&D investment has increased by 48% since 2020
China is 10th in the Global Innovation Index
China leads in volume of high-impact international journal publications and patent applications
China has achieved self-sufficiency in mature process nodes (28nm and above)
US-led export curbs on advanced chip manufacturing technology
Mention of specific technologies: FDSOI, RISC-V, and nanoimprint lithography
state_and_policy_content: State Council press briefing by Minister Yin Hejun
Policy incentives attracting capital to replicate mature products
Establishment of national computing power scheduling platforms
Focus on creating AI and manufacturing application scenarios
capital_accumulation_content: Capital attracted to 'low-level, redundant internal competition' due to policy incentives
Investment in emerging and disruptive technologies
labor_content: Mention of the number of researchers being the world's largest
Focus on the introduction of scientific talent
what_is_missing_for_theory_evaluation: Specifics on how the state's funding mechanisms (subsidies, guidance funds) работает exactly
Detailed data on the majorité of the private sector's role versus state-owned enterprises (SOEs) in these specific breakthroughs
Detailed labor market dynamics beyond the general mention of 'talent'
Detailed cost-benefit analysis of the technical pathways chosen
